page: Amazon Web Services c8i.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2175.0 placing it in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression at 34.3 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.34 GB/sec also rank in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is limited due to the 2-vCPU configuration, with stress-ng multi-core performance falling into the bottom 10% and Geekbench multi-core scores in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for both read and write operations is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

The c8i.large instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-bound workloads that rely heavily on single-threaded execution speed rather than massive parallelization or high memory capacity. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and the 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth may limit data-intensive transfers. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and small-scale computational tasks that benefit from modern Intel instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with x86_64 architecture. Operating at 3.9 GHz, it provides 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core, paired with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core scores in the top 10% and high single-threaded compression throughput. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak due to the low core count and resource density. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and lightweight compute workloads that do not require high memory or parallel processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor, 4.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.9 GHz processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server delivering strong single-core performance and DDR5 memory for lightweight, single-threaded processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
Strong single-core and single-threaded compression performance
AWS Nitro hypervisor with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for single-threaded applications and text processing
